page: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The processor includes 32 KB of L1 instruction/data cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA. There is no bundled local storage or GPU hardware included with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2701.79. Multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 21617.8 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 9778.0. Memory read bandwidth is average, reaching 1032.03 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 39.52 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, large-block memory write bandwidth is weak, registering in the bottom 25% at 22.78 GB/sec. For lightweight language model tasks, text generation using a small 135M model is strong at 273.29 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model is average at 838.26 tokens/sec.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core provides significant resource density for memory-bound workloads, offering qualitative cost efficiency for applications requiring large datasets in memory without needing proportional compute scaling. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local storage and weak large-block memory write speeds. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers like Redis, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v5 is an EASv5-family virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16 GB per core. Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it operates under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and strong text generation speeds for small language models, alongside average multi-core and memory read capabilities, though large-block memory write bandwidth is weak. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching layers.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM. It offers strong single-core performance and is designed for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32-16as_v5 features 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16 GB per core ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance and small-model text generation speeds
No bundled local storage, but compatible with Premium Storage
Suited for database operations, caching layers, and memory-heavy workloads
